title: The Danger of 'Absence Claims' on Food Labels
subtitle: 食品标签缺失声明的隐患

paragraphs.en: In recent years, the food industry has increased its use of labels. Whether the labels say 'non-GMO' or 'no sugar', or 'zero carbohydrates', consumers are increasingly demanding more information about what's in their food. One report found that 39 percent of consumers would switch from the brands they currently buy to others that provide clearer, more accurate product information.
paragraphs.zh: 近年来食品行业标签使用量激增。无论是“非转基因”、“无糖”还是“零碳水”，消费者越来越要求了解食品成分信息。一份报告发现，39%的消费者愿从现有品牌转向提供更清晰准确产品信息的品牌。

paragraphs.en: Food manufacturers are responding to the report with new labels to meet that demand, and they're doing so with an eye towards giving their products an advantage over the competition, and bolstering profits. This strategy makes intuitive sense. If consumers say they want transparency, tell them exactly what is in your product. That is simply supplying a certain demand.
paragraphs.zh: 食品生产商以新标签回应需求，同时着眼于让产品在竞争中占据优势并提升利润。这一策略直觉上合理：既然消费者要透明，告诉他们产品成分即可，这不过是满足需求。

paragraphs.en: But the marketing strategy in response to this consumer demand has gone beyond articulating what is in a product, to labeling what is NOT in the food. These labels are known as 'absence claims' labels, and they represent an emerging labeling trend that is detrimental both to the consumers who purchase the products and the industry that supplies them.
paragraphs.zh: 但回应消费者需求的营销策略已从说明产品“含有什么”演变为标注“不含什么”。这类标签称为“缺失声明”，代表一种新兴趋势，对购买产品的消费者和供应方均有害。

paragraphs.en: For example, Hunt's put a 'non-GMO' label on its canned crushed tomatoes a few years ago—despite the fact that at the time there was no such thing as a GMO tomato on the market. Some dairy companies are using the 'non-GMO' label on their milk, despite the fact that all milk is naturally GMO-free, another label that creates unnecessary fear around food.
paragraphs.zh: 例如Hunt's几年前在罐装碎番茄上贴“非转基因”标签，尽管当时市场根本不存在转基因番茄。一些乳业公司在牛奶上标注“非转基因”，而所有牛奶天然不含转基因，这是又一个制造不必要食品恐慌的标签。

paragraphs.en: While creating labels that play on consumer fears and misconceptions about their food may give a company a temporary marketing advantage over competing products on the grocery aisle, in the long term this strategy will have just the opposite effect. By injecting fear into the discourse about our food, we run the risk of eroding consumer trust in not just a single product, but the entire food business.
paragraphs.zh: 尽管利用消费者对食品的恐惧和误解制作标签，可能让公司在货架上暂时获得竞争优势，但长远来看效果恰恰相反。将恐惧注入食品讨论，我们面临的风险不仅是损害单一产品信任，更是侵蚀整个食品行业的公信力。

paragraphs.en: Eventually, it becomes a question in consumers' minds: Were these foods ever safe? By purchasing and consuming these types of products, have I already done some kind of harm to my family or the planet? For food manufacturers, it will mean damaged consumer trust and lower sales for everyone.
paragraphs.zh: 最终在消费者心中留下疑问：这些食品究竟是否安全？购买食用此类产品是否已对家人或地球造成伤害？对食品生产商而言，这意味着消费者信任受损，人人销量下滑。

paragraphs.en: And this isn't just supposition. A recent study found that absence claims labels can create a stigma around foods even when there is no scientific evidence that they cause harm. It's clear that food manufacturers must tread carefully when it comes to using absence claims. In addition to the likely negative long-term impact on sales, this verbal trick sends a message that innovations in farming and food processing are unwelcome.
paragraphs.zh: 这并非凭空推测。近期研究发现，即使没有科学证据表明有害，缺失声明标签也能给食品带来污名。显然，食品生产商在使用缺失声明时须格外谨慎。除了对销售的潜在长期负面影响，这种文字把戏还传递出农业和食品加工创新不受欢迎的信号。

paragraphs.en: Eventually leading to less efficiency, fewer choices for consumers, and ultimately, more costly food products. If we allow this kind of labeling to continue, we will all lose.
paragraphs.zh: 最终导致效率降低、消费者选择减少以及食品成本上涨。若纵容此类标签继续蔓延，我们将全盘皆输。
